Main Street Capital Access Atlas
Home Explore Illinois Methodology Limitations Sources Contribute

County-level public data

See where Main Street support may need attention

The atlas turns public county data into a cautious 0-100 support signal. Use it to start local questions, not to rank communities or make credit decisions.

For Local civic, CDFI, chamber, philanthropy, and small-business support teams.
80+ means High follow-up: several public signals point to possible unmet support needs.
Use Open the county brief, check data quality, then verify with local partners.
How to read a score

An 80 is not a grade. It means public data shows enough overlapping signals to justify local follow-up.

Full scoring method
  1. 80-100High follow-up
  2. 60-79Elevated
  3. 40-59Watch
  4. 0-39Lower signal
Map view

Support signal by county

Lower Higher

Darker color means a stronger public-data support signal. The map is a starting point for local verification, not a verdict.

Start with a county

Selected county

No county selected. Click the map or search above to view the local signal summary.

Prefer a table? Use the county table.

Score calibration

Most counties sit in the middle

Use the distribution to understand whether a county is unusual before drawing local conclusions.

0-25 151 4.7%
26-50 1,637 50.8%
51-75 1,428 44.3%
76-100 6 0.2%

Illinois launch focus

A curated first state view

Selected Illinois examples are grouped for a more focused state-level review.

Featured brief Cook, IL Signal 64.3 | Quality B 134,513 establishments 1,139 FDIC branches | 13.3% poverty signal Illinois county Winnebago, IL Signal 63.5 | Quality B 6,286 establishments 63 FDIC branches | 16.0% poverty signal Illinois county Peoria, IL Signal 62.0 | Quality B 4,168 establishments 48 FDIC branches | 15.4% poverty signal Featured brief Lake, IL Signal 61.5 | Quality B 20,076 establishments 152 FDIC branches | 8.1% poverty signal Featured brief DuPage, IL Signal 57.3 | Quality B 34,121 establishments 252 FDIC branches | 6.4% poverty signal Illinois county St. Clair, IL Signal 55.6 | Quality B 4,973 establishments 68 FDIC branches | 13.6% poverty signal Illinois county Champaign, IL Signal 54.3 | Quality B 4,237 establishments 71 FDIC branches | 18.0% poverty signal Illinois county Sangamon, IL Signal 50.3 | Quality B 4,925 establishments 76 FDIC branches | 12.9% poverty signal Illinois county Adams, IL Signal 49.3 | Quality B 1,787 establishments 32 FDIC branches | 12.3% poverty signal Illinois county McLean, IL Signal 48.0 | Quality B 3,481 establishments 48 FDIC branches | 13.0% poverty signal Illinois county McDonough, IL Signal 47.3 | Quality B 611 establishments 17 FDIC branches | 19.9% poverty signal Illinois county Washington, IL Signal 26.6 | Quality B 364 establishments 13 FDIC branches | 7.8% poverty signal
Open the Illinois spotlight
Main Street Capital Access Atlas

Public data to start better conversations about small-business support. Built independently; no private data.

This project is built independently using public data. It is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by any financial institution, government agency, or data publisher.
